rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers and helper declarations refer to valid AADL features or previously declared AGREE constants/variables with matching types.

When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', identify and correct the first invalid reference or helper declaration on the same line, or remove the clause if it cannot be resolved.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or previously declared constants/variables; avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Ensure all AGREE scalar type declarations use valid names: int, real, and bool; avoid references to undefined or misnamed types.
When generating AGREE contracts, validate that property references and data types match those defined in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes.
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE contracts; avoid referencing subcomponent ports or implementation-specific elements in type-level annexes.
For type-level contracts, place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes with concrete bounds or named constants.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not reference unresolved property definitions or constants in AGREE contracts without first ensuring they are declared in the model.
Do not write guarantees that depend on implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
AGREE validator diagnostics about property definitions not being resolvable
Component type and implementation structure in AADL
